title: synth_ice40 -dsp crashes when SB_MAC16 is manually instantiated
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

The attached example uses SB_MAC16 to instantiate a simple 32 bit counter, which works as intended:

```
yosys -p 'read_verilog -sv blinky.v; synth_ice40 -top blinky -json blinky.json'
```

[dsp-inference-crash.zip](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/files/6950321/dsp-inference-crash.zip)

However if -dsp is added, yosys crashes:

```
yosys -p 'read_verilog -sv blinky.v; synth_ice40 -top blinky -json blinky.json -dsp'
...
2.25. Executing ICE40_DSP pass (map multipliers).
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
  what():  dict::at()
Aborted (core dumped)
```

This is on the nightly from 20210802.

## Expected behavior

It should be possible to do a combination of manual instantiation of SB_MAC16 and automatic DSP block inferencing. 

## Actual behavior

yosys crashes.
code_blocks:
yosys -p 'read_verilog -sv blinky.v; synth_ice40 -top blinky -json blinky.json -dsp'
...
2.25. Executing ICE40_DSP pass (map multipliers).
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::out_of_range'
  what():  dict::at()
Aborted (core dumped)
